zotero.abstractNote: We present Human3R, a novel framework for high-fidelity 3D human reconstruction from sparse, unconstrained, and potentially noisy images. Unlike existing methods that rely on multi-view consistency or template-based priors, Human3R leverages a generative 3D prior to recover detailed geometry and appearance. Our approach effectively handles challenging poses, occlusions, and varying lighting conditions, enabling robust reconstruction in diverse real-world scenarios. We demonstrate that Human3R achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard benchmarks and generalizes well to in-the-wild data.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: The Fourteenth International Conference on Learning Representations
